  • Security and Stability: Robust system security and stable authorization protocols ensure data integrity.
  • Bot Integration: Seamless connection with automation bots facilitates smooth task execution.
  • Customizable and Documented: Easy task modelling and accessible documentation simplify audit processes and maintenance.

Time Savings and Efficiency

AFC significantly reduces the time required for complex financial closing processes:

  • Automated Calculations: AFC streamlines complex financial calculations and reconciliations, automating tasks that previously required extensive manual effort. This automation reduces the risk of errors and accelerates the completion of financial activities.
  • Faster Financial Closing: By automating repetitive and time-consuming tasks, AFC shortens the overall financial closing cycle. Tasks that used to take days can now be completed in a matter of hours, allowing finance teams to focus on analysis and strategic planning.
  • Real-Time Data Processing: AFC enables real-time data processing and consolidation, ensuring that financial data is accurate and up to date. This immediacy supports faster decision-making and enhances the agility of the finance function.
  • Reduced Manual Intervention: The elevated level of automation within AFC means that less manual intervention is needed. Automated workflows and task triggers ensure that processes are executed efficiently and consistently, freeing up valuable time for finance professionals to engage in higher-value activities.
  • Improved Accuracy and Compliance: Automation within AFC reduces the likelihood of human error, leading to more accurate financial statements and reports. This increased accuracy supports compliance with regulatory requirements and enhances the overall integrity of financial data.

Streamlining Financial Processes

AFC redefines the way we handle financial closings:

  • Manual vs. Automated: Automated tasks in AFC vastly outperform manual processes, providing unparalleled efficiency.
  • Secure and Reliable: AFC offers a secure and stable environment for all financial closing activities.
  • User-Friendly and Customizable: Easy task maintenance, parametrization, and modelling enhance workflow optimization.
  • Growing Adoption: Increasing numbers of users and finance areas are adopting AFC, demonstrating its effectiveness and scalability.

The successful implementation of AFC at SAP was made possible through the collaborative efforts of our dedicated team members and the product team. Together, we have navigated this transformative journey and look forward to further advancements in our financial closing processes.

Our journey to implement SAP Advanced Financial Closing has been extensive but incredibly rewarding. The transition to AFC marks a significant milestone in our ongoing commitment to innovation and efficiency. We are excited to share the benefits of AFC with our customers and partners, demonstrating our thought leadership and dedication to transforming financial closing processes. We invite you to join us in embracing this innovative tool and experience the enhanced efficiency, automation, and user experience that AFC offers. Together, we can continue to drive innovation and achieve new heights in financial excellence. Thank you for your continued support and trust in SAP.

entry.title: Deactivate Credit Limit Management for TRM Financial Transactions in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Problem Description:

In S/4HANA Public Cloud, Treasury and Risk Management (TRM), when a financial transaction is being created (e.g. a financial transaction of SAP pre-delivered product type 55A is being created), the credit limit management for counterparty risk is automatically active for this financial transaction (the checkbox ‘CP Risk Active’ is automatically selected on tab Default Risk Limit), and sometimes the “annoying” warning message K-788 'Limit management was not updated' shows up.

Requirement:

The requirement is to deactivate the warning message K-788  'Limit management was not updated' when financial transaction is created or checked, and also deactivate the credit limit management for counterparty risk for financial transactions.

Solution:

1. Access app Financial Objects Substitution Rules - Default Risk Data.

2. Choose Create Rule.

3. On the dialog box Create Rule, choose appropriate Business Context and Event, and then choose Create.

In this example, the objective is to deactivate the credit limit management for financial transactions of product category 550, therefore we choose Business Context Money Market: Default Risk Data and Event Money Market: Default Risk Data.

The full list of Business Context and Event for transaction management is as below. Please choose the appropriate Business Context and Event according to the product category for which you would like to deactivate credit limit management.

Business Context

Event

Example of Related Product Categories

Derivatives: Default Risk Data

Derivatives: Default Risk Data

620, 760

Foreign Exchange: Default Risk Data

Foreign Exchange: Default Risk Data

600

Money Market: Default Risk Data

Money Market: Default Risk Data

530, 540, 550, 560, 580

Security Transaction: Default Risk Data

Security Transaction: Default Risk Data

010, 020, 030, 040, 042, 160

Trade Finance: Default Risk Data

Trade Finance: Default Risk Data

850, 860

4. On the next screen, enter the Rule Name, Description, and then specify the precondition and substitution.

Example:

  • Precondition: FinancialInstrProductCategory Equal To 550
  • Substitution: TrsyFinObjCtptyRiskActiveCode Substitute with Constant 0, with ‘Overwrite’ checkbox selected.

This rule means that credit limit management for counterparty risk will be deactivated for financial transaction of product category 550.

5. Save and Activate the rule. Make sure it is in status ‘Active’.

Result:

With this rule activated, the credit limit management for counterparty risk will be deactivated for newly created financial transaction of product category 550 (checkbox ‘CP Risk Active’ is not selected), and the warning message K-788 will not show up when saving or checking the financial transaction of product category 550.

Appendix:

To use this app Financial Objects Substitution Rules - Default Risk Data, please assign a business role containing appropriate business catalog to your user. More details (including how to transport rule between systems) could be found from help document https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/e5ec5859d8e54df98492d80564a734c0/08f3f64e67ea4ea1a785a29d94f85f13.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Automatic Credit Score Calculation in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
1. Credit Management

In SAP, credit management is a function within Financial Supply Chain Management (FSCM) that oversees and regulates customer credit limits to promote timely payments and minimize the risk of bad debt. Mainly these 3 scope items are responsible for Overall credit management function:

Basic Credit Management (BD6)

Advanced Credit Management (1QM)

Credit Agency Integration (1RY)

2. Automatic Credit Score Calculation

Automatic Credit Score Calculation is a feature of advanced credit management that streamlines the process of setting credit scores through automation. We will explore how the system is set up and operates to automatically determine credit score.

Please note: This blog is intended for individuals who already have a fundamental understanding of basic credit management. Automatic Credit score Calculation is part of 1QM scope item.

There will be a separate blog for Automatic Credit Limit Calculation.

3. Related Configuration

Some configurations are managed through the configuration node (CBC or Manage Your Solution), while others are defined directly via various SAP Fiori Apps.

 

Process Step

App Name/ Config

Remarks

Credit Formula for Score and CL

Define Formulas [CBC]

Just the structure, values are filled with Fiori App

Rule for Scoring and Credit Limit, Rating procedure assignment

Create Rule for Scoring and Credit Limit Calculation [CBC]

 

Formula Parameters are created

Maintain Formula Parameters (App)

Formula Parameters are created and assigned to score formula group and limit formula

Formula is created

Manage Formula in Credit Management (App)

Formula is created (code is used previous one).
Simulation can be done

Valuation is assigned against the rules

Manage Credit Management Rules (app)

 

Credit Details are updated in Customer Master

Manage Credit Account

 

  • Define Formulas (CBC)

Here, we are only setting up the code and framework for the Credit Score Formula. The logic will be incorporated later in the Manage Formula in Credit Management App. This same formula is assigned during the configuration process in the "Create Rule for Scoring and Credit Limit Calculation" section.

  • Define Rating Procedure (CBC)

It is an optional configuration if you an agency which cannot find then you can create your own here.

You also need to define the rating results within your rating procedure, similar to how an agency assigns ratings like A, AA, AAA, B, etc or just A, B, C, D, E, etc.

In this blog, we've used the standard rating procedure, 'creditsafe,' for calculating credit scores.

 

  • Create Rule for Scoring and Credit Limit Calculation (CBC)

 

Here, we create a rule which is used for credit score and Credit Limit calculation. The formula is assigned to a rule within both the Score folder and the Credit Limit folder. We will focus Credit score folder in this blog.

Credit Score Folder:

Here, we assign credit score formula that we had created under ‘Define Formulas’

The rating procedure is like credit agencies, and several such agencies are available in the standard system. You also have the option to create custom rating procedures and assign them to rules for determining credit score.

Note: This Credit Score Rule is assigned with Business Partner (Customer) in Manage Credit Account App. Please refrain from using the Maintain Business Partner App for credit management purposes. It is recommended to use the Manage Credit Account App instead. Using the Maintain Business Partner App may not yield the desired outcomes in some processes within Credit Management.

 

  • Manage Formula in Credit Management (App)

You can create Credit Score Formula from Template or from scratch (create). We will create from scratch.

It is assigned to the formula you previously created in the configuration step "Define Formula."

Threshold and Rounding are applicable to credit limit calculations and will be explained in detail in another blog.

Scoring Formula Tab:

Multiple methods can be defined for calculating your credit score formula. In our approach, we have considered the following factors:

COUNTRY_REGION, CREDIT_GROUP, COFACE_RATING, CREDITSAFE_RATING, and CASH_RATIO

When assigning weight, there are two sections: Parameter Group (General, External Information) and Formula Parameter (COUNTRY_REGION, CREDIT_GROUP, CREDITSAFE_RATING and CASH_RATIO), each requiring specific weight allocation. You must ensure that the total for each individual group sums to 100, and then the overall parameter group weight also totals 100, as shown in the screenshot above.

 

Penalty Parameter

The penalty parameter is used to apply negative adjustments, such as deducting points, to address minor fraud or other issues in the overall credit score calculation. However, if the fraud is significant, you may opt to stop extending credit to that customer. In such cases, the Exit parameter can be used to set a zero or minimal credit limit, as explained in the next point.

 

Exit Parameter

The Exit parameter is typically used in cases of bankruptcy, major fraud, or similar situations where you would no longer extend credit to the customer. The Exit parameter sets a fixed credit score for the customer, ranging from 1 to 100, regardless of the system's calculated score.

This fixed score overrides the calculated score and becomes the final value assigned to the customer. Consequently, this will indirectly affect the credit limit calculation if you are using risk classes. Risk classes can be automated based on the score assigned to customers, and these risk classes can then be used in automatic determination of credit limit.

The calculation and logic for score calculation can be seen in the simulation in the blog below.

We have used the following Formula Parameters in our Credit Score Formula, which we will explore in detail in the subsequent step:

  1. Country/Region)
  2. Credit Group
  3. Coface Rating
  4. Creditsafe Rating
  5. Affidavit
  6. Fraud
  7. Bankruptcy

 

Credit Limit Simulation:

We can simulate the credit score calculation for a specific business partner to verify the functionality of our formula. You can expect the same results when performing calculations for the same customer in the Manage Credit Account App.

The system initially calculated the score as 64 (22+28+14) and applied a penalty of -40 points (10+30). However, due to the Exit formula parameter (i.e., Bankruptcy), the system overwrote the score to 1, which became the customer's final assigned score.

You can see the calculation for each parameter according to its assigned weight, including any negative adjustments for the penalty parameter. Additionally, the exit condition (parameter) can be observed, with its assigned rating overriding the overall calculation. Ultimately, the score assigned to the customer will be the one set in the exit parameter, such as 1.

 

  • Maintain Formula Parameters App

Numerous formula parameters are already available; ideally, you should use the standard ones, but it is also possible to create your own formula parameters in case business need.

Formula parameters can be categorized into two types:

  1. Formula Parameter for Score Calculation: Assigned to the Parameter Group for Score Formula (used in credit score formulas).
  2. Formula Parameter for Credit Limit Calculation: Assigned to the Parameter Group for Limit Formula (used in credit limit formulas).

In this blog, we will focus solely on the 'Parameter Category for Score Formula'.

Let's explore the specifics of below Formula Parameters which we have used in our Score Formula

  1. Country/Region
  2. Credit Group
  3. Coface Rating
  4. Creditsafe Rating
  5. Cash Ratio
  6. Affidavit
  7. Fraud
  8. Bankruptcy

If you create a custom Formula Parameter, it must be assigned to the appropriate folder where it belongs. In this blog, we have not created any custom formula parameters for score calculation, as the standard ones are sufficient.

 

COUNTRY_REGION (Country/Region)

 

CREDIT_GROUP (Credit Group)

CREDITSAFE_RATING (Creditsafe Rating)

Similarly, we can see other formula parameter.

  • Manage Credit Management Rules

Here, valuation is mapped to specific rules.

We will explore the rules that are pertinent to the Credit Score Formula:

  1. Formula: Parameter Evaluation
  2. General: Country/Region Risk

  

  • Formula: Parameter Evaluation

Assign values to the parameters that you use to calculate the scoring in credit management.

Pre-delivered formula parameters are available. You can define more formula parameters in this app: Maintain Formula Parameters 

 

We maintain several necessary formula parameters here for our credit score calculation. We assign variable values to these formula parameters, such as the credit group (whatever group you have created in the standard). For each credit group, you can specify an internal result (weightage/valuation). The internal result maintained here corresponds to the weightage you provided for the same formula parameter in the "Manage Formula in Credit Management" app. And that is how it works and contribute to credit score calculation.

There are six columns in Formula: Parameter Evaluation, each serving a different purpose:

  1. Formula Parameter
  2. Parameter Value
  3. Number (Parameter Value)
  4. Amount (Parameter Value)
  5. Date (Parameter Value)
  6. Interim Result

For each Formula Parameter, only one of the following columns will be populated—Parameter Value, Number (Parameter Value), Amount (Parameter Value), or Date (Parameter Value)—depending on the nature of the data, as indicated by the column names.

 

  • General: Country/Region Risk

Specify which country or region is associated with which country/region risk. This rule can be utilized for scoring in credit management. The corresponding country/region risk for each business partner is also displayed in the Manage Credit Accounts app.

 4. Master Data and Calculation Process in Manage Credit Account App

Assignment of Calculation Rule and Credit Check Rule

 

Credit scores are calculated at the business partner level, while credit limits are determined at the segment level. In this discussion, we'll focus on how Credit Score and Risk Class are determined. For information on credit limit calculation, stay tuned for an upcoming blog.

Note: The Calculation Rule (ZCRLIMIT) that we created in Create Rule for Scoring and Credit Limit Calculation configuration serves as the connection among all the configurations and settings implemented for calculating the credit score.

Maintenance of Fraud, Affidavit and Bankruptcy:

Imp: This data is assigned with X (not 1, 2, 3) under Parameter value in Maintain Credit Management Rules App

Financial Statement Data E.g. Cash Ratio

An import feature is available and should be used to update the customer's financial statement data.

 

  • Credit Score calculation.

The validity (score valid to) is determined by the configuration settings in the "Create Rule for Scoring and Credit Limit Calculation" configuration.

In the top right corner, there is a "Calculate Score" button, which becomes available in the Scoring Tab when in edit mode. You can use this button to calculate the customer's credit score.

 

  • Risk Class Determination:

Along with score calculation, the system will also determine the risk class based on the configuration set in "Create Risk Classes."

You can also manage various other parameters in Manage Credit Accounts App for your business partner from a credit management perspective, such as Credit Group, First Year of Customer Relationship, and more.

Note that I have only discussed the items we utilized from the perspective of our blog; there are many other aspects of master data relevant to credit management that are not covered here. This blog is intended for those who have a solid understanding of basic credit management.

 

Key Links:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/ba31db0b263a438c8640ec3731d0b640.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/6c529513913f427d8d73bd359cb74ca5.html

--------------------

Stay tuned for upcoming blogs on a variety of topics.

entry.title: Automatic Credit Limit Calculation in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
1. Credit Management

In SAP, credit management is a function within Financial Supply Chain Management (FSCM) that oversees and regulates customer credit limits to promote timely payments and minimize the risk of bad debt. Mainly these 3 scope items are responsible for Overall credit management function:

Basic Credit Management (BD6)

Advanced Credit Management (1QM)

Credit Agency Integration (1RY)

 

2. Automatic Credit Limit Calculation

Automatic Credit Limit Calculation is a feature of advanced credit management that streamlines the process of setting credit limits through automation. We will explore how the system is set up and operates to automatically determine credit limits.

Please note: This blog is intended for individuals who already have a fundamental understanding of basic credit management. Automatic Credit Limit Calculation is part of 1QM scope item.

 

Additionally, there is a separate blog for Automatic Credit Score Calculation available:

Automatic Credit Score Calculation in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

3. Scenario

We are going to calculate credit limit based on three condition such as

  • Last three months sales of the company code with the customer
  • Collateral
  • Risk Adjustment (Calculated Credit Limit adjustment based on Risk Class of the customer)

 

4. Related Configuration

Some configurations are managed through the configuration node (CBC or Manage Your Solution), while others are defined directly via various SAP Fiori Apps.

 

Process Step

App Name/ Config

Remarks

Credit Formula for Score and CL

Define Formulas [CBC]

Just the structure, values are filled with Fiori App

Rule for Scoring and Credit Limit, Rating procedure assignment

Create Rule for Scoring and Credit Limit Calculation [CBC]

 

Formula Parameters are created

Maintain Formula Parameters (App)

Formula Parameters are created and assigned to score formula group and limit formula

Formula is created

Manage Formula in Credit Management (App)

Formula is created (code is used previous one).
Simulation can be done

Valuation is assigned against the rules

Manage Credit Management Rules (app)

 

Credit Details are updated in Customer Master

Manage Credit Account

 

 

  • Define Formulas (CBC)

Here, we are only setting up the code and framework for the Credit Limit Formula. The logic will be incorporated later in the Manage Formula in Credit Management App. This same formula is assigned during the configuration process in the "Create Rule for Scoring and Credit Limit Calculation" section.

 

  • Create Rule for Scoring and Credit Limit Calculation (CBC)

Here, we create a rule which is used for credit score and Credit Limit calculation. The formula is assigned to a rule within both the Score folder and the Credit Limit folder. We will focus Credit Limit folder on this blog.

 

 Credit Limit Folder:

Here, we assign Segment and credit limit formula that we had created under ‘Define Formulas’

 Note: The Score Folder and Rating Procedure selection are primarily needed for credit score calculation. We will cover these in another blog titled Automatic Credit Score Calculation in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 Note: This Credit Score Rule is assigned with Business Partner (Customer) in Manage Credit Account App. Please refrain from using the Maintain Business Partner App for credit management purposes. It is recommended to use the Manage Credit Account App instead. Using the Maintain Business Partner App may not yield the desired outcomes in some processes within Credit Management.

  

  • Manage Formula in Credit Management (App)

 

You can create Credit Limit Formula from Template or from scratch (create). We will create from scratch.

It is assigned to the formula you previously created in the configuration step "Define Formula."

Threshold and Rounding will be detailed in the sections below. Please pay close attention.

 

Credit Limit Formula Tab:

Change mode.

Multiple methods can be defined for calculating your credit limit formula. In our approach, we have considered the following factors:

The credit formula is divided into two sections: 1. Limit Demand and 2. Limit Adjustment.

 

Limit Demand:

We calculate the credit limit based on the total sales value from the last three months, plus the collateral value. For example, if your sales were Rs. 700 and collateral was valued at Rs. 300, then your credit limit would be calculated as Rs. 1000.

 

Limit Adjustment:

- If the Risk class is A (No Default Risk), the credit limit will be twice the Limit Demand (Rs. 1000 * 2 = Rs. 2000).

- If Risk Class is B (Low Default Risk), the credit limit will be 1.5 times the Limit Demand (Rs. 1000 * 1.5 = Rs. 1500).

- If Risk Class is D (High Default Risk), the credit limit will be 0.5 times the Limit Demand (Rs. 1000 * 0.5 = Rs. 500).

The credit score formula calculates the credit score, which automatically fetches the risk class based on the configuration (Create Risk Classes). This illustrates the integration between credit score calculation and credit limit adjustment, where the risk class plays a critical role in determining the adjusted credit limit.

 

Under Limit Demand: Operand 1 and Operand 2 are Formula Parameter and Result Parameter is a Text. The Result Parameter is a descriptive text you need to provide when defining it, and it can be anything that makes sense for the process. In our case, we have named it ‘Credit Limit Calculation’.

Under Limit Adjustment: Operand 1 is a Formula Parameter and Operand 2 is the Result Parameter from Limit Demand.

 

We have incorporated the following Formula Parameters in our Credit Limit Formula, which we will explore in detail in the subsequent step:

  1. ZSALE_H_3M (Sales from the Last 03 Months)
  2. COLLATERALS (Collaterals)
  3. RISK_ADJUSTMENT (Risk Adjustment)

Note: It is necessary to establish Formula Parameters prior to creating the Credit Limit Formula in the Manage Formula in Credit Management (App).

 

Credit Limit Simulation:

We can simulate the credit limit calculation for a specific business partner and segment to verify the functionality of our formula. You can expect the same results when performing calculations for the same customer in the Manage Credit Account App.

 

You can learn about Limit Adjustment, Rounding, and Threshold in the Manage Credit Management Rules Section.

 

  • Maintain Formula Parameters App

Numerous formula parameters are already available; ideally, you should use the standard ones, but it is also possible to create your own formula parameters.

 

Formula parameters can be categorized into two types:

  1. Formula Parameter for Score Calculation: Assigned to the Parameter Group for Score Formula (used in credit score formulas).
  2. Formula Parameter for Credit Limit Calculation: Assigned to the Parameter Group for Limit Formula (used in credit limit formulas).

 

In this blog, we will focus solely on the 'Parameter Category for Limit Formula'.

 

Let's explore the specifics of these three Formula Parameters

  1. ZSALE_H_3M (Last 03 Months Sales)
  2. COLLATERALS (Collaterals)
  3. RISK_ADJUSTMENT (Risk Adjustment)

If you create a custom Formula Parameter, it needs to be assigned to the appropriate folder. For example, we created a Formula Parameter called ZSALE_H_3M for credit limit calculation, which we then assigned to the Parameter Category for Limit Formula.

 

ZSALE_H_3M (Last 03 Months Sales)

 

COLLATERALS (Collaterals)

 

RISK_ADJUSTMENT (Risk Adjustment)

 

  • Manage Credit Management Rules

Here, valuation is mapped to specific rules.

We will explore the rules that are pertinent to the Credit Limit Formula:

  1. Formula: Credit Limit Rounding Rule
  2. Formula: Credit Limit Threshold
  3. Formula: Risk Adjustment

 

Formula: Credit Limit Rounding Rule

You can define it by Risk Class. In this instance, I have left the Risk Class field blank, meaning it applies to all Risk Classes in the system. With an amount of 100 and a rounding position of 2, along with a Rounding Rule of Up (Round Up), this configuration means if the system calculates a credit limit amount as Rs. 7056, it will round up to Rs. 7100.

 

Formula: Credit Limit Threshold

Continued screen shot

Here, we set the minimum and maximum values for credit limit calculations. If the minimum value is set at Rs. 6000, then the system will assign a credit limit of Rs. 6000, even if the calculated limit falls below Rs. 5999. Likewise, the system will not exceed the maximum value assigned here.

Note: You can establish multiple conditions and designate various lower and upper credit limit values based on factors such as Segment, Country, Risk Class, and Credit Group.

 

Formula: Risk Adjustment

Adjust the credit limit of a business partner depending on the risk class and credit segment.

Input a Risk Class to adjust the credit limits for. If you want the adjustment to apply to all credit segments, leave the Credit Segment field blank. The credit limit for the relevant business partners will be multiplied by the value entered in the Risk Adjustment field. Entering a factor of 1 in the Risk Adjustment field will leave the credit limit unchanged.

Entering a factor greater than 1 will increase the credit limit.

Entering a factor less than 1 will decrease the credit limit.

 

This is utilized in the 'Manage Formula in Credit Management App'. Please refer to that section for a detailed explanation.

 

 5. Master Data and Calculation Process

Master data such as:

  • Calculation Rule

Credit scores are computed at the business partner level, while credit limits are calculated at the segment level.

Note: The Calculation Rule (ZCRLIMIT) serves as the connection among all the configurations and settings implemented for calculating the credit limit.

 

  • Segment Tab (go to details): Credit Limit Calculation

Validity is derived from the configuration settings in Create Rule for Scoring and Credit Limit Calculation (CBC).

In the top right corner, you'll find a button marked in yellow "Calculate Credit Limit." This button appears in the segment section when in edit mode. Once you calculate the credit limit for the customer and segment combination, the remaining process will proceed as usual.

 

  • Collateral

Items like Collateral, Insurance, etc., can be maintained either at the Business Partner Level or the Segment Level, depending on the business scenario. It is feasible to maintain these values at both levels simultaneously.

Note that I have only discussed the items we utilized from the perspective of our blog; there are many other aspects of master data relevant to credit management that are not covered here. This blog is intended for those who have a solid understanding of basic credit management.Top of Form

 

Key Links:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/ba31db0b263a438c8640ec3731d0b640.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/8a35ffa389004b3a9f9b8b22ef81e3a1.html

 

----------------

Stay tuned for upcoming blogs on a variety of topics.

entry.title: Deactivate Credit Limit Management for TRM Financial Transactions in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Problem Description:

In S/4HANA Public Cloud, Treasury and Risk Management (TRM), when a financial transaction is being created or checked, the credit limit management for counterparty risk is automatically active for the financial transaction (the checkbox ‘CP Risk Active’ is selected on tab Default Risk Limit), and sometimes the “annoying” warning message K-788 ‘Limit management was not updated’ shows up.

Requirement:

The requirement is to deactivate the warning message K-788  ‘Limit management was not updated’ when financial transaction is created or checked, and also deactivate the credit limit management for counterparty risk for financial transactions.

Solution:

1. Access app Financial Objects Substitution Rules - Default Risk Data.

2. Choose Create Rule.

3. On the dialog box Create Rule, choose appropriate Business Context and Event, and then choose Create.

In this example, the objective is to deactivate the credit limit management for financial transactions of product category 550, therefore we choose Business Context Money Market: Default Risk Data and Event Money Market: Default Risk Data.

The full list of Business Context and Event for transaction management is as below. Please choose the appropriate Business Context and Event according to the product category for which you would like to deactivate credit limit management.

Business Context

Event

Example of Related Product Categories

Derivatives: Default Risk Data

Derivatives: Default Risk Data

620, 760

Foreign Exchange: Default Risk Data

Foreign Exchange: Default Risk Data

600

Money Market: Default Risk Data

Money Market: Default Risk Data

530, 540, 550, 560, 580

Security Transaction: Default Risk Data

Security Transaction: Default Risk Data

010, 020, 030, 040, 042, 160

Trade Finance: Default Risk Data

Trade Finance: Default Risk Data

850, 860

4. On the next screen, enter the Rule Name, Description, and then specify the precondition and substitution.

Example:

  • Precondition: FinancialInstrProductCategory Equal To 550
  • Substitution: TrsyFinObjCtptyRiskActiveCode Substitute with Constant 0, with ‘Overwrite’ checkbox selected.

This rule means that credit limit management for counterparty risk will be deactivated for financial transaction of product category 550.

5. Save and Activate the rule. Make sure it is in status ‘Active’.

Result:

With this rule activated, the credit limit management for counterparty risk will be deactivated for newly created financial transaction of product category 550 (checkbox ‘CP Risk Active’ is not selected), and the warning message K-788 will NOT show up when saving or checking the financial transaction of product category 550.

Appendix:

To use this app Financial Objects Substitution Rules - Default Risk Data, please assign a business role containing appropriate business catalog to your user. More details (including how to transport rule between systems) could be found from help document https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/e5ec5859d8e54df98492d80564a734c0/08f3f64e67ea4ea1a785a29d94f85f13.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Supply Chain in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408
entry.content:
This blog post provides an overview of innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition release 2408 for the line of business Supply Chain. 

Table of Contents

Order Promising

  • Dynamic Third-Party Order Processing with Advanced ATP (‌‌‌‌▶️demo video)
  • CDS View Schedule Line With Open Requirement Quantity in Sales and Distribution

Inventory Management

  • Manage Physical Inventory Count app: Download–Upload Option (‌‌▶️demo video)
  • Manage Tolerances for Physical Inventory Differences (‌‌▶️demo video)
  • Special Stock E & K,  restricted-use stocks in Transfer Stock Apps (‌‌▶️demo video) 

Warehousing Management

  • Warehouse-Driven Split of the Outbound Delivery Order (▶️demo video)
  • Generation of Packaging Items (▶️demo video)
  • Partial Goods Receipt Posting (▶️demo video)

Transportation Management

  • Supplier Return with Transportation Management  (▶️demo video) + ...

More Information / Additional links

First watch the video from my teammate Markus Oertelt in which he presents his release highlights for supply chain management. 

Video 1: Watch the highlights by Markus Oertelt

 

Order Promising

Dynamic Third-Party Order Processing with Advanced ATP

Third-Party Order Processing (TPOP) with advanced ATP (aATP) now enables the processing of order items that are shipped to a subsidiary or plant. You can also use a new Business Add-In (BAdI) to modify the substitution result from Alternative-Based Confirmation (ABC) to, for example, exclude substitutions from being used for confirmations and influence their priority.

In addition to the existing shipment type Third-Party Direct Shipment, you can now define a sourcing profile for third-party substitution strategies with the new shipment type Bought-In Item. This means the third-party order items shall be shipped to an own plant or subsidiary (instead of to the customer directly). In the sales order, the corresponding subitems get item category CBAB and also lead to a full confirmation in the availability check.

Using the new BAdI, Enriched Substitution Result in aATP, you can adapt the substitution result found by ABC. You can remove found substitutes from the list (for example, because their source of supply shall not be used for confirmations) and influence the priority of substitutions used for confirmations.

For the above, the Configure Substitution Strategy app has been enhanced with Advanced Configuration options (Sourcing Profile, Follow-Up Strategies, and Business Add-In) at substitution strategy level. Furthermore, a new subscreen (Follow-Up Strategies) for the configuration of follow-up strategies has been added. You can use follow-up strategies to enable additional confirmations to be used to fulfill outstanding requested quantity for requirements. After the availability of a requirement has been checked, the follow-up strategy is applied to any remaining quantity that could not be confirmed by the substitution strategy (or the preceding follow-up strategy if multiple follow-up strategies have been assigned to the substitution strategy.) If multiple follow-up strategies are defined, they are applied in the sequence defined by the ordinal number in the Configure Substitution Strategy app.

Discover the intricacies of third-party order processing through three detailed cases that Andreas Krause showcase in the video below: delegation to supplier for fulfillment, dynamic fulfillment decision (starting at 2:21 min), and a scenario in which we do not want that our supplier send the goods directly to our customer (starting at 6:00 min).

Video 2: Dynamic Third-Party Order Processing with Advanced ATP

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

CDS View Schedule Line With Open Requirement Quantity in Sales and Distribution

The latest 2408 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition brings an exciting innovation in the area of extensibility. We released the new CDS View Schedule Line With Open Requirement Quantity in Sales and Distribution (CDS View Name I_SDScheduleLineWthOpenRqmtQty) which allows you to read for single open requirements in sales and distribution. From a technical standpoint, the data is read from table VBBE, which contains these entries.
This new CDS view opens up a world of possibilities for businesses looking to integrate their planning solutions with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. By consuming the data provided by the CDS view, companies can now seamlessly integrate their planning processes with their SAP S/4HANA Cloud environment.

Figure 1: CDS view 'Schedule Line With Open Requirement Quantity in Sales and Distribution'

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

 

Inventory Management

Manage Physical Inventory Count app: Download–Upload Option

The Manage Physical Inventory Count app has been enhanced. A new feature to download and upload Excel files for stock counting allow customers with 3rd-party warehouses to provide printouts to their staff. Once this kind of paper-based physical inventory process is completed, the results can be uploaded back into the system as an Excel file. Watch the next video in which I show you the new Excel download/upload option in Manage Physical Inventory Count app.

Video 3: Manage Physical Inventory Count app - download and upload Excel files

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

Manage Tolerances for Physical Inventory Differences 

Now you can maintain tolerance groups for the posting of physical inventory differences to define the maximum value difference a user is allowed to post in physical inventory. For this configuration step, you define tolerance groups and assign user to the tolerance groups.

Figure 2: Configuration Step 'Manage Tolerances for Physical Inventory Differences''

Watch the video below in which I show you the configuration step and its impact on the physical inventory count.

Video 4: Manage Tolerances for Physical Inventory Differences

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

Special Stock E & K,  restricted-use stocks in SAP FIORi apps for performing stock changes 

You can use different stock types and special stock types to perform stock changes. The following table gives you an overview about the stock types and special stock types that are supported by the SAP Fiori apps Manage Stock, Transfer Stock - In-Plant, and Transfer Stock - Cross-Plant. Blue highlighted stock types are newly supported since SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. In detail: 

  • The special stock types Order Stock (E) and Consignment Stock (K) have been added to the Manage Stock and the Transfer Stock - Cross-Plant apps.
  • You can now also display the stock type Restricted-Use Stock in the Transfer Stock - In-Plant and Transfer Stock - Cross-Plant apps.

Figure 3: Supported Stock Types and Special Stock Types in SAP Fiori apps for Goods Movement

In the next video I show you the use of the new stock types and special stock types in the SAP Fiori apps for performing stock changes.

Video 5: Use of the stock types and special stock types that are newly supported in the SAP Fiori apps 'Manage Stock', 'Transfer Stock - In-Plant', and 'Transfer Stock - Cross-Plant'

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

 

Warehousing Management

Check out the blog post Warehouse Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Release – What’s New in which Christian Ries shares the innovations for Warehouse Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release.

In this blog post I highlight 3 innovations from Warehouse Management that my colleague Markus Oertelt demonstrated in our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series, and share the demo videos with you.

Warehouse-Driven Split of the Outbound Delivery Order

Now you can split packed and unpacked items, quantities, or handling units (HUs) from an existing outbound delivery order into a new outbound delivery order.
Use this feature to remove parts of an outbound delivery order that, for example, are not ready to be shipped or for which there is no space on the transport, by splitting those parts into a new outbound delivery order.
You can split the outbound delivery order either before or after warehouse processing has started with created or confirmed warehouse tasks. Access this feature through the Warehouse Monitor. Two new functions are available in the Warehouse Monitor for the following nodes:

  • Outbound Delivery Order --> Outbound Delivery Order Item --> More Methods --> Split Open Qty into new ODO
  • Documents --> Handling Unit --> More methods --> Split into new ODO

Watch the next video in which Markus Oertelt shows the warehouse-driven split of the outbound delivery order through two different use cases in the system:

  1. Use case - Complete picking is not possible
  2. Use case - Lack of space in the truck

Video 6: Warehouse-Driven Split of the Outbound Delivery Order

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

Generation of Packaging Items

For packed outbound deliveries packing material items can be generated automatically. The packing materials are posted goods issue in Warehouse Management (WM) and in Inventory Management (MM-IM). Generated items can be billing relevant. Items are generated with goods issue or with ready for shipping.

In the next video Markus Oertelt shows the new feature in the system and how to set it up in configuration. 

Video 7: Generation of Packaging Items

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

Partial Goods Receipt Posting

Now, you can post goods receipt for one or several handling units (HUs) that belong to the same delivery. You can post goods receipt for parts of the inbound delivery that are packed into HUs (Partial Goods Receipt). 
You can access this feature through the Warehouse Monitor. Two new functions are available on the level of the Handling Unit node:

  • Goods Receipt
    This function posts goods receipt for one or more HUs.
  • Reverse Goods Receipt
    This function reverses goods receipt for one or more HUs.

Watch the next video in which Markus Oertelt shows the new feature in the system.

Video 8: Partial Goods Receipt Posting

This feature is also available when you use radio frequency (RF) devices to carry out warehouse activities.

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

 

Transportation Management

Exciting product updates coming your way for Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408. I want to encourage you to read the latest blog post from Markus Oertelt 

  • Transportation Management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 

that includes an amazing system demo about Supplier Return with Transportation Management and many more interesting topics.

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

 

More Information / Additional links

You will find a collection of SAP S/4HANA Cloud product centric blog posts from Markus Oertelt and me here:

  •  SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Supply Chain - The Link Collection
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Manufacturing & Supply Chain - The Link Collection
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Asset Management – The Link Collection
  • Discrete Industries in SAP S/4HANA Cloud - The Link Collection

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn and @GerhardWelker.

 

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series! 

From July 8 to July 12, we hosted a series of 20 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find a xx-minute-long recording and a presentation for LoB/Industry/Topic as well as 19 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime. 

🔎Find All of Our Assets 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below: 

 

️Thanks for reading this blog post. Stay tuned!

(🔙 Table of Contents)

entry.title: Leveraging Key User Extensibility in BRIM - Transfer data from Billable Items to FI documents
entry.content:
Have you ever wondered how to transfer an information from your incoming billable item to an FI document in your BRIM solution?

In my blog article, I’ll will show you how to extend your BRIM solution using the Custom Fields app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public and Private Edition)!

Content:

  • The sample requirement and solution approach
  • Creation and configuration of the sample custom field
  • Conclusion
  • Appendix

The sample requirement and solution approach

In a BRIM implementation with Convergent Invoicing (CI) and Contract Accounting (FI-CA) it is a common requirement to transfer a custom information from the billable item (BIT) to the item of an FI document.

The Key User Extensibility app Custom Fields allows you to extend your BRIM solution with additional, custom fields and to copy the information from a source to a target object. When you create a custom field using the app, you choose a Business Context, which determines which object is extended. The configuration of your custom fields allows you to enable Business Scenarios, a business scenario controls the automatic transfer of data. The value in the custom field is always copied from source to target, even if the source field is empty.

The diagram below provides an overview of the active Business Contexts and Business Scenarios you need to forward an information from the billable item to the FI document.

Overview active Business Contexts and Business Scenarios

Creation and configuration of the sample custom field

The following section provides basic information on how to make a custom information from your billable item object available in the subsequent Convergent Invoicing and Contract Accounting objects and finally in your ACDOCA document in FI.

1. Launch the Custom Field app (F1481) and create a new field. Choose business context Convergent Invoicing: Billable Items. In our example, the field is labelled MTBitText, it’s of type Text with a length of 20 characters. The identifier is automatically proposed by the app.

Initial field creation

2. The detailed configuration of a custom field allows you to enable several features. In our example, we want to include the new field in the relevant Fiori apps, add it to the API for billable item creation and forward it to the billing document item (Business scenario: Billable Items to Billing Document Item).

Once you have completed the configuration, publish the new field.

The screenshots below show the sample configuration:

Enabled user interfaces

Business Scenario

SOAP API

3. Once the publishing process is complete, you will notice that your field has also been automatically created for the business context Convergent Invoicing: Billing Document Item. Don’t worry, this is the intended feature, not a bug! Since we activated the business scenario Billable Items to Billing Document Item, the app generates the custom field for the target business context as well.

Now proceed with configuring your field across the remaining objects. Start with the context Convergent Invoicing: Billing Document Item and finish with Accounting: Journal Entry Item. Use the diagram from the first chapter as a guide to identify the necessary business contexts and scenarios.

Remember to activate not only the business scenarios, but also the user interfaces. Depending on your specific requirements, you may also need to enable your custom field for OData services and Form Templates.

Remark and warning:

  • You can include additional fields to the total records transferred from FI-CA to the general ledger. To do this, you must activate the Contract Accounting Business Partner Item to Journal Entry Item or Contract Accounting General Ledger Item to Journal Entry Item business scenarios.
  • Think twice if you really need to have the information in FI! If you update totals records with additional fields, the number of totals records and journal entry items may increase considerably.

After completing all the configurations, make sure to publish your custom field for all relevant business contexts.

Business contexts

4. Now it’s time to test the configuration.

4a. Create test billable items that include a value in your custom field.
The new custom field is available on the billable item and the value is shown.

Manage Billable Items

4b. Execute the billing and invoicing process in Convergent Invoicing.

The value of your custom field is copied by the business scenario from source to target.

Manage Billing Documents

Manage Invoicing Documents

4c. Transfer the reconciliation key from Contract Accounting to FI.

As usual, you can customize the grid layouts and include your field in them.

Maintain Reconciliation Key (FPF3)

Manage Journal Entries

Conclusion

From my perspective, the key user extensibility apps offer a powerful yet user-friendly solution for custom extensions in your daily work as a BRIM consultant. Additionally, they help keep the core of your BRIM solution clean, ensuring that each change remains fully compatible with future releases.

Now it’s up to you: Explore the capabilities and features of Key User Extensibility in BRIM!

I hope you find this blog post useful and that you can benefit from this feature in your projects!

 

Appendix

Apps:

  • F1481 - Custom Fields

Documentation:

  • Documentation for F1481-Custom Fields app
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud – BRIM – Extend your system
  • SAP S/4HANA – Convergent Invoicing Extensibility (OP)
  • How to transfer existing fields in a custom include to Custom Fields app

 SAP Convergent Invoicing SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management SAP Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable

entry.title: Safe (and happy) Cycling!
entry.content:
We’ve all become experts at constant movement, whether we’re joining new groups, coaching our friends, or planning our next big adventure.

SAP innovations are delivered in cycles that are familiar to you, putting solutions in your hands that simplify everyday work, increase your effectiveness, and secure your success. Because we know that your finance and HR teams collaborate as they plan and reconcile budgets with employee position and financing requirements, we are releasing continuous improvements to our platform that give you easier, real-time controls. And yes, we've built in AI-powered insights, recommendations, and automation. 

SAP’s public sector solution uses SAP SuccessFactors HCM alongside SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP Business AI, for end-to-end management of strategic, financial, HR and operational decisions for budgeting and forecasting. In the upcoming release cycles (2H2024 and 1H2025) you’ll be able to manage position budget control with all the compliance and automation that you’ve come to expect from your industry-minded neighbors here at SAP. See how people are managed, and if positions are funded, alongside grant reservation and commitments. Look forward to conversations with colleagues that shape policy and efficiently run programs, and make informed, fast financial and operational decisions with live access to intuitive dashboards and simulation tools. Speed along your chosen path to people assignments, finish payroll and accounting runs, and complete the financial reporting of budgets and grants; proactively monitor employee costs, use automated anomaly detection and prevention to mitigate fraud risks and reduce losses. Adopt these features at your own pace.

Subscribe to keep moving ahead: SAP SuccessFactors What's New and SAP S/4HANA Cloud 

It’s a comprehensive and unified approach that increases any organizations’ stewardship of taxpayer money. No matter if your agency has 500 people, 5,000 people, or is larger: we help governments and not-for-profits make data driven decisions that carry their mission forward.

entry.title: Update your Public Cloud Expertise - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Product Expert Training HY2 2024
entry.content:
For all our partners and internals: Register now for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Product Expert Training starting 23rd Sept!

This exclusive 7-day event has a packed agenda with live, expert-led webinars on topics covering the entire Public Cloud ERP and, as always, offers you the chance to fine-tune your product knowledge by seeing hands-on product demos and learning all there is to know about new ERP features which drive customer success. Benefit from previews of upcoming roadmap features and engage with our product experts via the interactive Q&A at each webinar.

Make sure your Learning Hub License is up to date, log-in to SAP Learning, and get registered!

You’re a customer and want to learn about new features? Many of the topics have been covered in the latest SAP S/4HANA Cloud Early Release Series, simply check out this blog post to find the replays of your favourite sessions.
